description: The change-approval gate accepts an approval from the change's own author, defeating independent review.
body: Failure mode: the pipeline checks that an approval exists but not that the approver differs from the author, so an author approves their own change and it deploys to a regulated system unreviewed. Detection signals: a deploy proceeds with author and sole approver being the same principal; the gate counts approvals without comparing identities; emergency changes routinely self-approve. Do not count an approval without verifying approver-author distinctness. Safer fallback: require the gate to reject any change whose only approver is its author, bind the approval to the exact artifact, and route break-glass self-approvals to a retroactive human review.
